original_hypothesis.title: AgentToken._totalSupply uses uint128 casting while balances are uint256 (supply conservation invariant break / integration risk)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AgentToken stores _totalSupply as uint256 but updates it via uint128 casts in _mint/_burn. If mint amounts exceed uint128 (or cumulative supply exceeds uint128), totalSupply can diverge from sum(_balances), breaking ERC20 invariants and potentially causing downstream math errors (swap thresholds, tax thresholds, external integrations).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if current config keeps supplies small, this is a latent correctness defect. totalSupply is used in _autoSwap to compute swapThresholdInTokens; a truncated totalSupply can materially change swap behavior.
deployment_assumptions: Factory/admin might configure maxSupply values that, after multiplying by 1e18, can exceed type(uint128).max or approach it enough to risk truncation in upgrades/migrations
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No on-chain enforcement that lpSupply/vaultSupply (in base units) * 1e18 <= type(uint128).max; only checks maxSupply (pre-decimal) <= uint128 in _processSupplyParams

proof_of_signal.pattern: _totalSupply += uint128(amount) and _totalSupply -= uint128(amount) despite _totalSupply being uint256

preconditions: Mint/burn amounts (in wei units) exceed uint128.max OR cumulative _totalSupply exceeds uint128.max at some point
Token is used by integrations relying on accurate totalSupply (thresholds, analytics, DEX listings)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Balances can exceed supply; swapThreshold computed from truncated _totalSupply
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No require(amount <= type(uint128).max) in _mint/_burn; _processSupplyParams checks maxSupply in pre-decimal units only

analysis_questions: Is maxSupply in supplyParams expressed in whole tokens and later multiplied by 1e18 (yes); does the uint128 check account for this multiplication (no)?
Can any code path mint after initialize (not shown here) that could push supply over uint128.max?
Does swap threshold behavior depend on totalSupply and could truncation cause unexpected autoswaps or disable swaps?
validation_requirements: Compute boundary: type(uint128).max / 1e18 and compare to allowed maxSupply values
Fuzz initialize with large maxSupply values to see if it passes _processSupplyParams but breaks _mint casting
Check if any external tooling assumes ERC20 invariants that are violated

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If misconfigured with very large supplies, totalSupply truncates while balances remain high; thresholds computed from totalSupply become wrong, causing autoswap to trigger too early/late or never, and external protocols may treat the token as having much smaller supply.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Moderate to high depending on integrations; could break tax swap logic and DEX pricing assumptions
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All token holders and any protocol depending on totalSupply

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing invariant test: sum(balances)==totalSupply and boundary tests around uint128 limits

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor for totalSupply discrepancies vs sum of known balances in indexers

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
